source_intake_record.raw_question_text: As shown in the figure, in rectangle $ABCD$, $AD=2\sqrt{3}$ and $AB=5$. Let $M$ be a point on $CD$. $\triangle ADM$ is folded along the straight line $AM$ to obtain $\triangle ANM$. If $AN$ bisects $\angle MAB$, what is the length of $CN$?
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text:
\textbf{Solution}: As shown in the diagram, draw a perpendicular line from point N to CD, intersecting $CD$ at point E. \\
From the fold, we know: $DM=MN$, \\
$DA=AN=2\sqrt{3}$, \\
$\angle DAM=\angle NAM$ \\
Because AN bisects $\angle MAB$, \\
Therefore, $\angle NAM=\angle BAN$ \\
Therefore, $\angle DAM=\angle NAM=\angle BAN$ \\
Because $\angle DAB=90^\circ$, \\
Therefore, $\angle DAM=30^\circ$, \\
$\angle DAN=60^\circ$ \\
Therefore, $DM=AD\cdot\tan30^\circ=2\sqrt{3}\times \frac{\sqrt{3}}{3}=2$ \\
Therefore, $MN=DM=2$ \\
Because $\angle DAN+\angle DMN=180^\circ$, \\
$\angle CMN+\angle DMN=180^\circ$ \\
Therefore, $\angle CMN=\angle DAN=60^\circ$ \\
Therefore, $NE=MN\cdot\sin60^\circ=2\times \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}=\sqrt{3}$, \\
$ME=1$ \\
Therefore, $CE=5-2-1=2$ \\
Therefore, in right triangle $\triangle ECN$, by the Pythagorean theorem, we get: \\
$CN=\sqrt{{(\sqrt{3})}^{2}+{2}^{2}}=\sqrt{3+4}=\boxed{\sqrt{7}}$

field_audit_records.before_value: As shown in the figure, in rectangle $ABCD$, $AD=2\sqrt{3}$ and $AB=5$. Let $M$ be a point on $CD$. $\triangle ADM$ is folded along the straight line $AM$ to obtain $\triangle ANM$. If $AN$ bisects $\angle MAB$, what is the length of $CN$?
field_audit_records.after_value: As shown in the figure, in rectangle $ABCD$, $AD=2\sqrt{3}$ and $AB=5$. Let $M$ be a point on $CD$. $\triangle ADM$ is folded along the straight line $AM$ to obtain $\triangle ANM$. If $AN$ bisects $\angle MAB$, what is the length of $CN$?

field_audit_records.before_value:
\textbf{Solution}: As shown in the diagram, draw a perpendicular line from point N to CD, intersecting $CD$ at point E. \\
From the fold, we know: $DM=MN$, \\
$DA=AN=2\sqrt{3}$, \\
$\angle DAM=\angle NAM$ \\
Because AN bisects $\angle MAB$, \\
Therefore, $\angle NAM=\angle BAN$ \\
Therefore, $\angle DAM=\angle NAM=\angle BAN$ \\
Because $\angle DAB=90^\circ$, \\
Therefore, $\angle DAM=30^\circ$, \\
$\angle DAN=60^\circ$ \\
Therefore, $DM=AD\cdot\tan30^\circ=2\sqrt{3}\times \frac{\sqrt{3}}{3}=2$ \\
Therefore, $MN=DM=2$ \\
Because $\angle DAN+\angle DMN=180^\circ$, \\
$\angle CMN+\angle DMN=180^\circ$ \\
Therefore, $\angle CMN=\angle DAN=60^\circ$ \\
Therefore, $NE=MN\cdot\sin60^\circ=2\times \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}=\sqrt{3}$, \\
$ME=1$ \\
Therefore, $CE=5-2-1=2$ \\
Therefore, in right triangle $\triangle ECN$, by the Pythagorean theorem, we get: \\
$CN=\sqrt{{(\sqrt{3})}^{2}+{2}^{2}}=\sqrt{3+4}=\boxed{\sqrt{7}}$

variants.title: MM-Math 开放题
variants.rewritten_question_text: As shown in the figure, in rectangle $ABCD$, $AD=2\sqrt{3}$ and $AB=5$. Let $M$ be a point on $CD$. $\triangle ADM$ is folded along the straight line $AM$ to obtain $\triangle ANM$. If $AN$ bisects $\angle MAB$, what is the length of $CN$?
variants.expected_answer:
\textbf{Solution}: As shown in the diagram, draw a perpendicular line from point N to CD, intersecting $CD$ at point E. \\
From the fold, we know: $DM=MN$, \\
$DA=AN=2\sqrt{3}$, \\
$\angle DAM=\angle NAM$ \\
Because AN bisects $\angle MAB$, \\
Therefore, $\angle NAM=\angle BAN$ \\
Therefore, $\angle DAM=\angle NAM=\angle BAN$ \\
Because $\angle DAB=90^\circ$, \\
Therefore, $\angle DAM=30^\circ$, \\
$\angle DAN=60^\circ$ \\
Therefore, $DM=AD\cdot\tan30^\circ=2\sqrt{3}\times \frac{\sqrt{3}}{3}=2$ \\
Therefore, $MN=DM=2$ \\
Because $\angle DAN+\angle DMN=180^\circ$, \\
$\angle CMN+\angle DMN=180^\circ$ \\
Therefore, $\angle CMN=\angle DAN=60^\circ$ \\
Therefore, $NE=MN\cdot\sin60^\circ=2\times \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}=\sqrt{3}$, \\
$ME=1$ \\
Therefore, $CE=5-2-1=2$ \\
Therefore, in right triangle $\triangle ECN$, by the Pythagorean theorem, we get: \\
$CN=\sqrt{{(\sqrt{3})}^{2}+{2}^{2}}=\sqrt{3+4}=\boxed{\sqrt{7}}$
